evals.name: happy-path-full-codebase-audit
evals.prompt: audit my whole backend against our engineering rules and show me every violation — we keep merging code that breaks our own CLAUDE.md

assertions.text: lawkeeper skill is invoked (auto-discovery fires on full-codebase rule-audit intent)

Phase 1 resolves the effective rule set from the project's own harness (.claude/rules, ban-patterns.txt, CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md) with stricter-wins fallback to global doctrine — it does not hardcode a duplicate rule copy
Runs the bundled deterministic scanner (scripts/audit_scan.py, referenced via the resolved <skill-dir>, not a hardcoded absolute path) for the exact checks

Fans out semantic subagents for the judgment rules (DRY, layering, naming, SRP, security, performance, testing, SOLID, cleanup); reuses the project's installed .claude/agents/ review agents when present
Produces a report grouped by category and severity with file:line for every finding before fixing anything
Fixes are propose-confirm per finding — nothing is auto-rewritten without approval

evals.name: edge-conformance-check-claude-md
evals.prompt: does this project actually follow our CLAUDE.md? check the hard caps and the bans across the repo

assertions.text: lawkeeper skill is invoked (auto-discovery fires on 'does this follow CLAUDE.md' / 'check the hard caps and bans' intent)
Deterministic scanner reports the exact bans (lint suppressions, non-null !, empty catch, bare Error, secrets, inline types in scoped modules) and the file-line cap with file:line
Carve-outs are applied before reporting (test-file waivers, generated/migration exemptions) so documented exceptions are not flagged
Honest about coverage — if scoped to a subtree or the semantic pass is skipped, it says so in the report

evals.name: non-trigger-single-diff-review
evals.prompt: review the diff on my current branch before I open the PR — just the changes, looking for bugs

assertions.text: lawkeeper skill is NOT invoked — this is a single-diff review of changed code, not a full-codebase compliance sweep
Routing goes to /code-review (or full hackify Phase 5), which review the diff rather than auditing the whole tree against the rule set
Transcript notes lawkeeper's scope is a FULL-CODEBASE audit against documented engineering laws, not a per-PR diff review
